runs.text: Implement a simple program that returns a weekday from an integer input.
This exercise uses MiniJava; A toolkit built into the exercise. Pay attention to the exercise instructions!
Using nested if statements, determine the season based on the month and day.
Run a block of code again and again until
Remember to make sure that your loop will somehow terminate!
Determine a condition when the loop is active.
Increment the counter. This ensures that somehow, the terminating condition is met!
The factorial of a number n (n!)
Use a while loop to sum up several inputs until a condition is met.
Use a combination of loops to compute the sum of all integers smaller or equal to the integer given.
Write an algorithm to find all prime factors of a number.

runs.text: Frequently, when manipulating an array or reading data, you need to access all => Use for loops.
In this example, the average of the values of an int[] array is calculated
Implement helper methods for integer arrays.
Don’t Repeat Yourself: Write code once and reuse it by calling the method
Methods help divide a problem into smaller chunks:
Return type: What type of data does it return?
(Note: If no return type, it is “void”)
Method name: Always make sure it is clear what the method does.
Input: What goes into the method?
Code block: What is executed when method is called.
return: Return the return type specified above.
“Utility helpers”: Take input, process it, and throw it out
Can only interact with other static variables or variables passed into them

Return type violations (wrong type, or forgot “return”)
“return” too early: The code terminates at return
Tip: for( … : … ) is called an enhanced for-loop (the easier version). It iterates through the collection without using an index!

runs.text: Java has some pre-defined classes (e.g. String, int, etc)
A class has its attributes and its methods
private: The variable is only visible within this class.
public: The variable is visible everywhere.
=> What properties are given about the object?
Either initialize the attributes directly or in the constructor

runs.name: Courier New

runs.text: methods that retrieve private attributes from outside the class.
Setters: methods that change (set) private attributes of the class.
We can implement our own custom methods to the class, to manipulate its attributes, or to execute processes.
Methods describe the behavior of an object of the class.
It must be initialized with attributes.
Car porsche = new Car(Engine abc, Door[] def, Driver ben);

runs.text: We can interact with objects by using their class methods.
Duck, fish and zebra are all animals with their own attributes and methods too.
